chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, LARKANA Crl. Misc. Appln. No.S-22 of 2026. DATE OF HEARING ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF HON’BLE JUDGE 1. For orders on M.A.No.353/2026. 2. For orders on office objection as Flag ‘A’. 3. For orders on M.A.No.354/2026. 4. For hearing of main case. 26.01.2026 Mr. Abid @ Abdul Qadir Abro, advocate for the applicant. -.-.-.-.-.- 1. Granted. 2-4. The applicant has challenged order dated 29.12.2025 passed by the Additional Sessions Judge-V/Ex-Officio Justice of Peace, Larkana, under sections 22-A & 22-B, Cr.P.C, whereby he dismissed the applicant’s application for directions to register an FIR against the proposed accused. It was averred by the applicant that while she and her sister, namely, Mst. Khursheed, were cultivating their land, the proposed accused attacked them with ‘Sotas’ which resulted in a broken leg of Mst.Khursheed. However, after calling a preliminary report from the DSP Complaint Cell, the learned Additional Sessions Judge found that there was money dispute between the parties; that the applicant used to facilitate persons for withdrawal of Benazir Income Support Program money; in a similar transaction with the proposed accused there arose a dispute between them; and that Mst. Khursheed was in fact injured by a buffalo, not by the proposed accused. Learned counsel submits that the learned Additional Sessions Judge did not appreciate the certificate of injury issued by the Medico Legal Officer at Chandka Medical College, Larkana, in respect of the injury suffered by Mst. Khursheed. However, that certificate also goes against the applicant inasmuch as it states that the injury suffered by Mst.

Khursheed. However, that certificate also goes against the applicant inasmuch as it states that the injury suffered by Mst. Khursheed examined on 09.12.2025, had occurred 10 days back. Therefore, the impugned order does not call for interference. Resultantly application is dismissed in limine. Judge M Yousuf Panhwar/**
